children.heading: Period for awarding grant funds
children.label: a Period for awarding grant funds

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Unless otherwise specifically provided in an authorization, DOJ grant funds for a fiscal year shall remain available to be awarded and distributed to a grantee only in that fiscal year and the three succeeding fiscal years, subject to paragraphs (2) and (3). DOJ grant funds not so awarded and distributed shall revert to the Treasury.

children.heading: Treatment of reprogrammed funds
children.label: 2 Treatment of reprogrammed funds
children.text: DOJ grant funds for a fiscal year that are reprogrammed in a later fiscal year shall be treated for purposes of paragraph (1) as DOJ grant funds for such later fiscal year.

children.heading: Treatment of deobligated funds
children.label: 3 Treatment of deobligated funds
children.text: If DOJ grant funds were obligated and then deobligated, the period of availability that applies to those grant funds under paragraph (1) shall be extended by a number of days equal to the number of days from the date on which those grant funds were obligated to the date on which those grant funds were deobligated.

children.heading: Period for expending grant funds
children.label: b Period for expending grant funds
children.text: DOJ grant funds for a fiscal year that have been awarded and distributed to a grantee may be expended by that grantee only in the period permitted under the terms of the grant. DOJ grant funds not so expended shall be deobligated.

children.heading: Definition
children.label: c Definition
children.text: In this section, the term “DOJ grant funds” means, for a fiscal year, amounts appropriated for activities of the Department of Justice in carrying out grant programs for that fiscal year.

children.heading: Applicability
children.label: d Applicability
children.text: This section applies to DOJ grant funds for fiscal years beginning with fiscal year 2006.
